reasoning: As of October and early November 2025, gold is trading well above $3,700 per ounce, having broken the $3,850 barrier at one point. The technical support is identified in the $3,700s. To close below $3,200 at the end of 2025, gold would need to suffer a plunge of more than $500 (or >13-15%) within roughly 1.5 months. Given typical annualized gold volatility of around 15%, a 15% downward move in under 2 months equates to an extreme tail event (at least 2.5 to 3 standard deviations). Most institutional year-end forecasts (like UBS at $3,800, J.P. Morgan at $3,675) predict prices far above $3,200, and even the "bear case" by Savvy Wealth stands at 3,200. Thus, the probability of gold closing at $3,200 or more is exceedingly high. I assign the maximally permitted confidence of 0.95.
batch_queries: Will Gold close at $3,200 or more at the end of 2025?
